steps.title: Recall the tangent ratio
steps.content: In a right triangle, $\tan A = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}}$ — the side opposite angle $A$ over the side adjacent to angle $A$ (not the hypotenuse).

steps.title: Plug in the side lengths
steps.content:
Triangle ABC has a right angle at B, hypotenuse AC = 114, and angle C = $30^\circ$. Since the angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$, angle A = $180^\circ - 90^\circ - 30^\circ = 60^\circ$.
In a 30-60-90 triangle with hypotenuse 114: the side opposite $30^\circ$ (AB) is $114 \div 2 = 57$, and the side opposite $60^\circ$ (BC) is $57\sqrt{3}$.
$$\tan A = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{BC}{AB} = \frac{57\sqrt{3}}{57} = \sqrt{3} \approx 1.732$$
The answer is 1.732.
